checks.a_analysis: The output follows the structure of the source document and accurately represents the text present on the pages. The metadata title 'Map Imagery Photos Tables Screenshots Diagrams Data Charts Ff40' is filler text but is an allowed exception for AI-generated titles. There are no fabricated paragraphs or body text found.
The document contains several URLs (e.g., on pages 2, 13). I have verified the text elements in the JSON against the source PDF. No fabricated URLs, emails, or phone numbers were found.
Each of the 13 pages of the source PDF is represented in the JSON output through corresponding section headings or images.
There are no tables in the source document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The conversion fails to capture significant amounts of text content across several sections. On Page 3 (Outline), more than half of the sub-items and the final 'Future Priorities' item are missing. On Pages 8, 9, 11, and 12, the text labels associated with the graphical icons (e.g., 'ADDRESS NC STEERING COMMITTEE', 'NC ONEMAP CLOUD MIGRATION', 'NOAA LAND COVER PILOT') are entirely omitted. Additionally, the URLs on Page 2 and Page 13 are missing from the text content. This represents a systematic loss of more than 25% of the text in those sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 (Outline) sub-items: 'Data Governance', 'Public Access to GIS Data', 'Collaboration for Land Information', 'Intergovernmental Collaboration', 'Sharing Information and Knowledge', and 'Future Priorities'. Page 8 labels: 'ADDRESS NC STEERING COMMITTEE', 'WORKING GROUP FOR SEAMLESS PARCELS'. Page 9 labels: 'NC ONEMAP CLOUD MIGRATION', 'TAC INFRASTRUCTURE WORKING GROUP'. Page 11 labels: '2020 CENSUS UPDATES AND ASSISTANCE', 'NOAA LAND COVER PILOT', 'ELEVATION DATA', 'HYDROGRAPHY WORKING GROUP'. Page 12 labels: 'ESRI SPECIAL ACHIEVEMENT AWARD', 'ESRI ENTERPRISE LICENSE AGREEMENT', 'PROFESSIONAL MEETINGS'. Page 13 URL: 'www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in section 'Outline' (reading order 26) stops at 'Accomplishments'. The text elements for pages 8, 9, 11, and 12 (reading orders 69, 76, 90, 99) contain only the section titles, with no labels for the icons. The URL on page 13 is missing from the element list (reading order 108-112).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: Outline (Page 3), Collaboration for Data Governance... (Page 8), Collaboration for Public Access... (Page 9), Intergovernmental Collaboration... (Page 11), Sharing Information... (Page 12), Questions? (Page 13).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully captured matches the source document with no changes in meaning. Variations in vertical spacing/merging (e.g., names and titles on the front page) are acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: Several sections at the end of the document contain duplicate image elements for the NCDIT logo which appears only once on each slide in the source. Specifically, on Page 11, images at index 96 and 97 are duplicates. Page 12 contains three duplicate images at indices 105, 106, and 107. Page 13 contains two duplicate images at indices 111 and 112. This constitutes structural duplication of visual elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: The NCDIT logo appears exactly once at the bottom right of each slide (pages 3-13).
checks.b_evidence_output: Multiple image elements for the same logo: Page 11 (indices 96, 97), Page 12 (indices 105, 106, 107), Page 13 (indices 111, 112).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections for pages 11, 12, and 13; element indices 96-97, 105-107, 111-112.

checks.a_analysis: The heading hierarchy is mostly flat, with each slide title being assigned an H1 tag. Sub-headings like 'ORTHOIMAGERY PROGRAM' on Page 10 are correctly assigned H2. While flat, the hierarchy is consistent and navigable, which falls under acceptable adjustments for accessibility.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table corruption is possible.
The reading order matches the logical flow of the slides and the arrangement of elements within the slides (title, then content, then footer logos).

The bulleted list on Page 3 (Outline) is incorrectly converted. The output list only contains the first 5 top-level items and completely omits the sub-bullets under 'Accomplishments' as well as the final main bullet 'Future Priorities'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Bulleted list on Page 3 containing items: Executive Summary, About the Council, FY 20-21 Highlights, Goals, Accomplishments (with 5 sub-bullets), and Future Priorities.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list element at reading_order index 26 only contains 5 items: 'Executive Summary', 'About the Council', 'FY 20-21 Highlights', 'Goals', 'Accomplishments'.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains visible URLs on Page 2 (https://it.nc.gov/gicc-annual-report-FY-20-21) and Page 13 (www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback). These are completely missing from the text elements in the JSON output, although the Page 2 URL is identified in the provided OCR metadata.
checks.b_evidence_source: URLs visible on Page 2 (https://it.nc.gov/gicc-annual-report-FY-20-21) and Page 13 (www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback).
checks.b_evidence_output: The text for these URLs is absent from all paragraph or heading elements in the corresponding sections.

checks.a_analysis: The rendered HTML misinterprets decorative background elements as significant foreground content. In the source PDF on pages 4, 5, 6, and 7, there are faint grey circles and white bar designs in the background. In the HTML output, these are rendered as large, solid grey circles and semicircles and white blocks that take up substantial vertical space, disrupting the visual flow and organization of the information. This creates a cluttered and broken appearance that does not represent the original layout intent.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 4, 5, 6, and 7 of the source PDF show a light grey circle and bar background pattern behind the content boxes.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows large grey circles and bars rendered as primary page elements between the 'Highlights' sections.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections starting with 'Highlights: COVID Response' through 'Highlights: Infrastructure'

checks.a_analysis: There are no tables in the source PDF document or the rendered HTML to evaluate.

Several meaningful images are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the circular icons that accompany the text on pages 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12 are entirely absent. Additionally, the large photo on page 13 showing people raising their hands in a meeting ('Questions?' section) is also missing. While some images like the aerial photo and page 2 icons are present, the omission of thematic icons and a major photo represents a significant loss of visual information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Circular icons on pages 8-12 (e.g., map icon for Address NC, water drop for Hydrography) and the meeting photo on page 13.
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding sections in the HTML only contain text labels without the accompanying icons or photos.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Collaboration for Data Governance and Consistency' through 'Questions?'

checks.a_analysis: The NCDIT logo appears to be duplicated in multiple places where it was not duplicated in the source. For example, at the beginning of the document and between several sections, the logo is rendered twice in immediate succession. This is likely due to the logo being present in both the header/footer regions of sequential PDF pages, which the conversion process has concatenated.
checks.b_evidence_source: The NCDIT logo appears once on the cover page and once per page thereafter.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows two NCDIT logos appearing back-to-back at the start of the document and after the 'What We're Doing' section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Top of document and following the 'What We're Doing' section
